name: Spent halogenated solvents used in degreasing
notes: F001 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: The following spent halogenated solvents used in degreasing: Tetrachloroethylene, trichloroethylene, methylene chloride, 1,1,1-trichloroethane, carbon tetrachloride, and chlorinated fluorocarbons; all spent solvent mixtures/blends used in degreasing containing, before use, a total of ten percent or more (by volume) of one or more of the above halogenated solvents or those solvents listed in F002, F004, and F005; and still bottoms from the recovery of these spent solvents and spent solvent mixtures.

name: Spent halogenated solvents
notes: F002 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: The following spent halogenated solvents: Tetrachloroethylene, methylene chloride, trichloroethylene, 1,1,1-trichloroethane, chlorobenzene, 1,1,2-trichloro-1,2,2-trifluoroethane, ortho-dichlorobenzene, trichlorofluoromethane, and 1,1,2-trichloroethane; all spent solvent mixtures/blends containing, before use, a total of ten percent or more (by volume) of one or more of the above halogenated solvents or those listed in F001, F004, or F005; and still bottoms from the recovery of these spent solvents and spent solvent mixtures.

name: Spent non-halogenated solvents (xylene, acetone, etc.)
notes: F003 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (I)
hazardous_waste_description: The following spent non-halogenated solvents: Xylene, acetone, ethyl acetate, ethyl benzene, ethyl ether, methyl isobutyl ketone, n-butyl alcohol, cyclohexanone, and methanol; all spent solvent mixtures/blends containing, before use, only the above spent non-halogenated solvents; and all spent solvent mixtures/blends containing, before use, one or more of the above non-halogenated solvents, and a total of ten percent or more (by volume) of one or more of those solvents listed in F001, F002, F004, and F005; and still bottoms from the recovery of these spent solvents and spent solvent mixtures.

name: Spent non-halogenated solvents (cresols, nitrobenzene)
notes: F004 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: The following spent non-halogenated solvents: Cresols and cresylic acid, and nitrobenzene; all spent solvent mixtures/blends containing, before use, a total of ten percent or more (by volume) of one or more of the above non-halogenated solvents or those solvents listed in F001, F002, and F005; and still bottoms from the recovery of these spent solvents and spent solvent mixtures.

name: Spent non-halogenated solvents (toluene, MEK, etc.)
notes: F005 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (I), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: The following spent non-halogenated solvents: Toluene, methyl ethyl ketone, carbon disulfide, isobutanol, pyridine, benzene, 2-ethoxyethanol, and 2-nitropropane; all spent solvent mixtures/blends containing, before use, a total of ten percent or more (by volume) of one or more of the above non-halogenated solvents or those solvents listed in F001, F002, or F004; and still bottoms from the recovery of these spent solvents and spent solvent mixtures.

name: Wastewater treatment sludges from electroplating
notes: F006 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludges from electroplating operations except from the following processes: (1) Sulfuric acid anodizing of aluminum; (2) tin plating on carbon steel; (3) zinc plating (segregated basis) on carbon steel; (4) aluminum or zinc-aluminum plating on carbon steel; (5) cleaning/stripping associated with tin, zinc and aluminum plating on carbon steel; and (6) chemical etching and milling of aluminum.

name: Spent cyanide plating bath solutions
notes: F007 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Spent cyanide plating bath solutions from electroplating operations.

name: Plating bath residues (cyanide electroplating)
notes: F008 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Plating bath residues from the bottom of plating baths from electroplating operations where cyanides are used in the process.

name: Spent stripping and cleaning bath solutions (cyanide)
notes: F009 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Spent stripping and cleaning bath solutions from electroplating operations where cyanides are used in the process.

name: Quenching bath residues from oil baths (cyanide)
notes: F010 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Quenching bath residues from oil baths from metal heat treating operations where cyanides are used in the process.

name: Spent cyanide solutions from salt bath pot cleaning
notes: F011 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Spent cyanide solutions from salt bath pot cleaning from metal heat treating operations.

name: Quenching wastewater treatment sludges (cyanide)
notes: F012 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Quenching wastewater treatment sludges from metal heat treating operations where cyanides are used in the process.

name: Wastewater treatment sludges from conversion coating of aluminum
notes: F019 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludges from the chemical conversion coating of aluminum except from zirconium phosphating in aluminum can washing when such phosphating is an exclusive conversion coating process. Wastewater treatment sludges from the manufacturing of motor vehicles using a zinc phosphating process will not be subject to this listing at the point of generation if the wastes are not placed outside on the land prior to shipment to a landfill for disposal and are either: disposed in a Subtitle D non-hazardous landfill licensed or permitted by the state or federal government; or disposed in a landfill licensed or permitted to accept the waste described as F019.

name: Wastes from production/use of tri- or tetrachlorophenol
notes: F020 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastes (except wastewater and spent carbon from hydrogen chloride purification) from the production or manufacturing use (as a reactant, chemical intermediate, or component in a formulating process) of tri- or tetrachlorophenol, or of intermediates used to produce their pesticide derivatives. (This listing does not include wastes from the production of Hexachlorophene from highly purified 2,4,5-trichlorophenol.)

name: Wastes from production/use of pentachlorophenol
notes: F021 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastes (except wastewater and spent carbon from hydrogen chloride purification) from the production or manufacturing use (as a reactant, chemical intermediate, or component in a formulating process) of pentachlorophenol, or of intermediates used to produce its derivatives.

name: Wastes from use of tetra-, penta-, or hexachlorobenzene
notes: F022 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastes (except wastewater and spent carbon from hydrogen chloride purification) from the manufacturing use (as a reactant, chemical intermediate, or component in a formulating process) of tetra-, penta-, or hexachlorobenzene under alkaline conditions.

name: Wastes from equipment previously used for tri-/tetrachlorophenols
notes: F023 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastes (except wastewater and spent carbon from hydrogen chloride purification) from the production of materials on equipment previously used for the production or manufacturing use (as a reactant, chemical intermediate, or component in a formulating process) of tri- and tetrachlorophenols. (This listing does not include wastes from equipment used only for the production or use of Hexachlorophene from highly purified 2,4,5-trichlorophenol.)

name: Process wastes from chlorinated aliphatic hydrocarbon production
notes: F024 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Process wastes, including but not limited to, distillation residues, heavy ends, tars, and reactor clean-out wastes, from the production of certain chlorinated aliphatic hydrocarbons by free radical catalyzed processes. These chlorinated aliphatic hydrocarbons are those having carbon chain lengths ranging from one to and including five, with varying amounts and positions of chlorine substitution. (This listing does not include wastewaters, wastewater treatment sludges, spent catalysts, and wastes listed in §261.31 or §261.32.)

name: Condensed light ends / spent filters from chlorinated aliphatics
notes: F025 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Condensed light ends, spent filters and filter aids, and spent desiccant wastes from the production of certain chlorinated aliphatic hydrocarbons, by free radical catalyzed processes. These chlorinated aliphatic hydrocarbons are those having carbon chain lengths ranging from one to and including five, with varying amounts and positions of chlorine substitution.

name: Wastes from equipment previously used for chlorobenzene production
notes: F026 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastes (except wastewater and spent carbon from hydrogen chloride purification) from the production of materials on equipment previously used for the manufacturing use (as a reactant, chemical intermediate, or component in a formulating process) of tetra-, penta-, or hexachlorobenzene under alkaline conditions.

name: Discarded unused tri-/tetra-/pentachlorophenol formulations
notes: F027 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (H)
hazardous_waste_description: Discarded unused formulations containing tri-, tetra-, or pentachlorophenol or discarded unused formulations containing compounds derived from these chlorophenols. (This listing does not include formulations containing Hexachlorophene synthesized from prepurified 2,4,5-trichlorophenol as the sole component.)

name: Residues from incineration of F020-F023/F026-F027 soils
notes: F028 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Residues resulting from the incineration or thermal treatment of soil contaminated with EPA Hazardous Waste Nos. F020, F021, F022, F023, F026, and F027.

name: Wood preserving wastes – chlorophenolic formulations
notes: F032 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewaters (except those that have not come into contact with process contaminants), process residuals, preservative drippage, and spent formulations from wood preserving processes generated at plants that currently use or have previously used chlorophenolic formulations (except potentially cross-contaminated wastes that have had the F032 waste code deleted in accordance with §261.35 or potentially cross-contaminated wastes that are otherwise currently regulated as hazardous wastes, and where the generator does not resume or initiate use of chlorophenolic formulations). This listing does not include K001 bottom sediment sludge from the treatment of wastewater from wood preserving processes that use creosote and/or pentachlorophenol.

name: Wood preserving wastes – creosote formulations
notes: F034 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewaters (except those that have not come into contact with process contaminants), process residuals, preservative drippage, and spent formulations from wood preserving processes generated at plants that use creosote formulations. This listing does not include K001 bottom sediment sludge from the treatment of wastewater from wood preserving processes that use creosote and/or pentachlorophenol.

name: Wood preserving wastes – inorganic (arsenic/chromium)
notes: F035 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewaters (except those that have not come into contact with process contaminants), process residuals, preservative drippage, and spent formulations from wood preserving processes generated at plants that use inorganic preservatives containing arsenic or chromium. This listing does not include K001 bottom sediment sludge from the treatment of wastewater from wood preserving processes that use creosote and/or pentachlorophenol.

name: Petroleum refinery primary oil/water/solids separation sludge
notes: F037 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Petroleum refinery primary oil/water/solids separation sludge—Any sludge generated from the gravitational separation of oil/water/solids during the storage or treatment of process wastewaters and oily cooling wastewaters from petroleum refineries. Such sludges include, but are not limited to, those generated in oil/water/solids separators; tanks and impoundments; ditches and other conveyances; sumps; and stormwater units receiving dry weather flow. (See §261.31 for full exclusions.)

name: Petroleum refinery secondary (emulsified) separation sludge
notes: F038 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Petroleum refinery secondary (emulsified) oil/water/solids separation sludge—Any sludge and/or float generated from the physical and/or chemical separation of oil/water/solids in process wastewaters and oily cooling wastewaters from petroleum refineries. Such wastes include, but are not limited to, all sludges and floats generated in: induced air flotation (IAF) units, tanks and impoundments, and all sludges generated in DAF units. (See §261.31 for full exclusions.)

name: Multi-source leachate
notes: F039 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.31; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Leachate resulting from the disposal of more than one restricted waste classified as hazardous under subpart D of this part (leachate resulting from the disposal of one or more of the following EPA Hazardous Wastes and no other Hazardous Wastes retains its EPA Hazardous Waste Number(s): F020, F021, F022, F026, F027, and/or F028).

name: Bottom sediment sludge – wood preserving (creosote/PCP)
notes: K001 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Bottom sediment sludge from the treatment of wastewaters from wood preserving processes that use creosote and/or pentachlorophenol.

name: WWT sludge – chrome yellow and orange pigments
notes: K002 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of chrome yellow and orange pigments.

name: WWT sludge – molybdate orange pigments
notes: K003 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of molybdate orange pigments.

name: WWT sludge – zinc yellow pigments
notes: K004 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of zinc yellow pigments.

name: WWT sludge – chrome green pigments
notes: K005 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of chrome green pigments.

name: WWT sludge – chrome oxide green pigments
notes: K006 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of chrome oxide green pigments (anhydrous and hydrated).

name: WWT sludge – iron blue pigments
notes: K007 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of iron blue pigments.

name: Oven residue – chrome oxide green pigments
notes: K008 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Oven residue from the production of chrome oxide green pigments.

name: Distillation bottoms – acetaldehyde from ethylene
notes: K009 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Distillation bottoms from the production of acetaldehyde from ethylene.

name: Distillation side cuts – acetaldehyde from ethylene
notes: K010 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Distillation side cuts from the production of acetaldehyde from ethylene.

name: Bottom stream – wastewater stripper, acrylonitrile
notes: K011 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Bottom stream from the wastewater stripper in the production of acrylonitrile.

name: Bottom stream – acetonitrile column, acrylonitrile
notes: K013 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (R), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Bottom stream from the acetonitrile column in the production of acrylonitrile.

name: Bottoms – acetonitrile purification, acrylonitrile
notes: K014 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Bottoms from the acetonitrile purification column in the production of acrylonitrile.

name: Still bottoms – distillation of benzyl chloride
notes: K015 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Still bottoms from the distillation of benzyl chloride.

name: Heavy ends – carbon tetrachloride production
notes: K016 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Heavy ends or distillation residues from the production of carbon tetrachloride.

name: Heavy ends – epichlorohydrin purification column
notes: K017 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Heavy ends (still bottoms) from the purification column in the production of epichlorohydrin.

name: Heavy ends – ethyl chloride production
notes: K018 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Heavy ends from the fractionation column in ethyl chloride production.

name: Heavy ends – ethylene dichloride production
notes: K019 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Heavy ends from the distillation of ethylene dichloride in ethylene dichloride production.

hazardous_waste_description: Purification solids (including filtration, evaporation, and centrifugation solids), bag house dust and floor sweepings from the production of dithiocarbamate acids and their salts. (This listing does not include K125 or K126.)

name: Crude oil storage tank sediment – petroleum refining
notes: K169 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Crude oil storage tank sediment from petroleum refining operations.

name: Clarified slurry oil tank sediment – petroleum refining
notes: K170 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Clarified slurry oil tank sediment and/or in-line filter/separation solids from petroleum refining operations.

name: Spent hydrotreating catalyst – petroleum refining
notes: K171 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (I), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Spent hydrotreating catalyst from petroleum refining operations, including guard beds used to desulfurize feeds to other catalytic reactors (this listing does not include inert support media).

name: Spent hydrorefining catalyst – petroleum refining
notes: K172 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (I), (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Spent hydrorefining catalyst from petroleum refining operations, including guard beds used to desulfurize feeds to other catalytic reactors (this listing does not include inert support media).

name: WWT sludges – ethylene dichloride/vinyl chloride monomer
notes: K174 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludges from the production of ethylene dichloride or vinyl chloride monomer (including sludges that result from commingled ethylene dichloride or vinyl chloride monomer wastewater) but not including sludges that do not contain dioxin in a measurable concentration.

name: WWT sludge – vinyl chloride monomer (mercuric chloride catalyst)
notes: K175 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Wastewater treatment sludge from the production of vinyl chloride monomer using mercuric chloride catalyst in an acetylene-based process.

name: Nonwastewaters – dyes and/or pigments production
notes: K181 – 40 CFR 261 Subpart D (eCFR) §261.32; hazard code(s): (T)
hazardous_waste_description: Nonwastewaters from the production of dyes and/or pigments (including nonwastewaters commingled at the point of generation with nonwastewaters from other processes) that, at the point of generation, contain mass loadings of any of the constituents identified in 40 CFR 261.32 above the listed levels.
